title: Roasted Silky Australian Sea Bass Bake
description: A delicate sea bass dish originating from the temples of Australia, perfect for a cold monsoon night. The kombu-cold-extract brings an unmatched silkiness to the fish, while the spices add depth and warmth.
instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Rinse the sea bass and pat it dry with paper towels.
Place the sea bass on the prepared baking sheet, skin side down.
In a small bowl, mix together the kombu-cold-extract, ghee, ginger, garlic, lemongrass, and galangal.
Brush the mixture evenly over the sea bass, leaving a small border around the edges.
Pour the coconut milk over the sea bass, making sure it's fully submerged.
Sprinkle the sweet basil over the top of the sea bass.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Bake in the preheated oven for 12 minutes, or until the sea bass is cooked through and flakes easily with a fork.
Remove from the oven and let rest for 2 minutes before serving.
